rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF LELAND STANFORD JR. UNIVERSITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants The Board of Trustees of the Leland Stanford Jr. University, Sabrina Liang, Randy Livingston, Robert Wallace, Susan Weinstein, and Marc Tessier-Lavigne's anti-SLAPP motion to strike plaintiff's claims for injurious falsehood/trade libel and abuse of process in the Santa Clara complaint is granted. The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS SUSAN WEINSTEIN, MARC TESSIER-LAVIGNE, ROBERT WALLACE, RANDY LIVINGSTON, SABRINA LIANG, THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF LELAND STANFORD JR. UNIVERSITY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court rules as follows on defendants The Board of Trustees of the Leland Stanford Jr. University, Sabrina Liang, Randy Livingston, Robert Wallace, Susan Weinstein, and Marc Tessier-Lavigne's demurrer to the Complaint, No. 19-CV-347760. The demurrer to the tax fraud and money laundering cause of action based on Cal. Rev. & Tax Code, §23701, 26 U.S.C. 7201 and 7206, and Cal. Probate Code, § 18501 is sustained without leave to amend. "[A] private right of action exists only if the language of the statute or its legislative history clearly indicates the Legislature intended to create such a right to sue for damages." (Vikco Ins. Services, Inc. v. Ohio Indem. Co. (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 55, 62 (emphasis in original).) In this case, there is nothing in the cited statutes to indicate a private right of action. The statutes do not contain any language providing a private remedy or means of a member of the public to enforce their substantive operative provisions. The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREY KUKUSHKIN VS. MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint [Ccp 436]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANTS MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint [CCP 436]. Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa's motion to strike the fourth amended complaint, joined by defendant Jeffrey Linden, is denied. Although Plaintiff's fourth amended complaint technically was late-filed, the Court's April 23, 2019 order did not specify a deadline for plaintiff to file a further amended complaint, and defendants have not shown they were prejudiced by the 15-day delay. Here, as in Harlan v. Department of Transportation (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 868, "'the delay in filing was brief and inconsequential.'" (Id. at 872-874 [trial court had discretion to accept late-filed second amended complaint without requiring motion for leave to amend].) Defendants' alternative request that the Court condition leave to amend on payment of Defendants' fees for filing the motion to strike is denied. Counsel for plaintiff is reminded to submit courtesy copies of all of his filed and lodged papers with department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREY KUKUSHKIN VS. MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LINDEN's DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Jeffrey Linden's demurrer to the fourth cause of action in plaintiff's fourth amended complaint for unjust enrichment is sustained without leave to amend. "'[A]n action based on an implied-in-fact or quasi-contract cannot lie where there exists between the parties a valid express contract covering the same subject matter.' [Citation.] However, 'restitution may be awarded in lieu of breach of contract damages when the parties had an express contract, but it was procured by fraud or is unenforceable or ineffective for some reason.' [Citation.] Thus, a party to an express contract can assert a claim for restitution by 'alleg[ing in that cause of action] that the express contract is void or was rescinded.'" (Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 231.) Here, plaintiff expressly alleges in his verified complaint that the nominal defendant Venture Rebel, Inc. and defendant Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc. dba Medithrive, Inc. entered into a written Management Services Agreement "that VENTURE believes is controlling, the terms of which VENTURE acted under and represents MED THRIVE and VENTURE's agreement." (Fourth Amended Complaint 6 & Ex. A; see also id. 69.) He does not allege in the fourth cause of action that the MSA is void or was rescinded, but only, on information and belief, that "Defendants will assert that the MSA is not enforceable as an illegal contract under Federal law or an otherwise defective agreement." (Id. 85.) That allegation is insufficient under controlling authority cited in both of the Court's prior rulings: "Although a plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims that allege both the existence of an enforceable agreement and the absence of an enforceable agreement, that is not what occurred here. Instead, plaintiffs' breach of contract claim pleaded the existence of an unenforceable agreement and their unjust enrichment claim did not deny the existence or enforceability of that agreement. Plaintiffs are therefore precluded from asserting a quasi-contract claim under the theory of unjust enrichment." (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-1390 [affirming order sustaining demurrer to unjust enrichment claim without leave to amend]; accord, Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1370-1371 ["An unjust enrichment theory is inapplicable because [plaintiff] alleges the parties entered into express contracts"]; California Medical Ass'n, Inc. v. Aeta U.S. Healthcare of California, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 151, 170-174.) Plaintiff has already been given two opportunities to amend his complaint to overcome this deficiency, and has failed to do so. Accordingly, the demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Counsel for plaintiff is reminded to submit courtesy copies of all of his filed and lodged papers with department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HURST VS. ONE KINGS LANE LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 2, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH HURST's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal. Plaintiff Elizabeth Hurst's motion for dismissal is granted. No opposition filed, and good cause shown. Plaintiff has complied with the requirements of California Rule of Court 3.770 by filing a declaration setting forth in detail the consideration being given for the dismissal. The Court has reviewed in camera the settlement agreement between Plaintiff Elizabeth Hurst individually and Defendants One Kings Lane LLC and Bed Bath & Beyond Inc. The Court has not ruled on class certification and notice of the pendency of the action has not been provided to putative class members, and finds that the dismissal without prejudice of the class claims will not prejudice such class members. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order including with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)
